description: A large, rigid steel boom.  If attached to a frame it could be used to lift up to 20 metric tonnes.
A small, telescoping steel cantilever.  If attached to a frame it could be used to lift up to 3.5 metric tonnes.
A makeshift pallet lifter.  If attached to a frame it could be used to lift up to 0.5 metric tonnes.
A large, heavy, jagged metal disc for digging trenches.
//: made out of High-Strength Low-Alloy steel, which is used as structural steel automobiles and modelled as lc_steel

description: An extendable metal pylon used to replace a portable jack.  If mounted to a vehicle, it could be used to lift it up.
A kickstand to keep a bike from falling over.  You could use this to lean a small vehicle forward or backward to change a tire.
The control sticks taken from an excavator.  They allow the control of hydraulically controlled equipment.

//: Without this installed, no hydraulic equipment can function.  Can NOT be fabricated by the player due to its complicated and specialized nature.  Must be scavenged from existing vehicles
description: A mechanical pump for creating hydraulic pressure from mechanical energy, generated by an brushless electric motor.

//: Without this installed, no hydraulic equipment can function.  Can NOT be fabricated by the player due to its complicated and specialized nature.  Must be scavenged from existing vehicles
description: An electronically controlled valve system for diverting hydraulic pressure to hydraulically-controlled equipment
A universal mount for boom-and-stick arms consister of a heavy-duty hinge joint with attachment points for hydraulic rams for horizontal and lateral movement

The boom-and-stick arm of an excavator with hydraulic hoses and rams for moving the arm. At the end is an attachment point for various construction equipment. It can lift 3/4 metric tonnes or be used to suspend something

A metal bucket with stubby teeth, commonly seen on the ends of excavator arms. Able to move earth and soil much faster than a shovel. It's attached by a hinge, with attachment points for hydraulic rams
